articles.title: Tennis Player Says Line-Calling System Was Wrong
articles.summary: Elena Rybakina, a tennis player from Kazakhstan, played a match in Madrid, Spain. She was not happy because the machine that calls if a ball is in or out made a mistake. The machine said a serve by her opponent, Zheng Qinwen from China, was good, but Rybakina thought it was out. She asked the umpire to check the mark on the clay court, but the umpire said she could not. Rybakina said she does not trust the machine anymore. Last year, another player named Alexander Zverev also had a problem with the same machine. He took a picture of a ball mark and got a warning. Rybakina said the mark was right in front of the umpire, so it was frustrating. She still won the match 4-6, 6-4, 6-3.

articles.title: Oil Prices Rise as Peace Talks Stall
articles.summary: Oil prices went up because the United States and Iran stopped talking about peace. The US wanted to send people to Pakistan to talk with Iran, but the US president said no. This is important because a waterway called the Strait of Hormuz is closed because of the war. Many ships carrying oil and gas go through this strait. When it is closed, there is less oil for everyone, so prices go up. Oil is used to make many things like fuel for cars and even plastic bags. Experts say if the strait stays closed for long, many products will become more expensive. People who trade oil are waiting to see if the conflict really ends before they believe prices will go down.

articles.title: A War Far Away Makes Things More Expensive for a Family
articles.summary: A war between the US, Israel, and Iran started on February 28. This war is far away from the UK, but it is making things more expensive for families there. Fuel prices have gone up a lot. For example, filling a car with petrol costs about £14 more, and diesel costs about £27 more. Naomi is a mum who lives in Chorley, Lancashire. She has a daughter named Riziah who is 10 years old and has serious health problems. Riziah needs to go to the hospital in Liverpool often, which is more than 30 miles away. The family uses a van for these trips. Now, filling the van costs almost £130, which is £30 more than before. Naomi is also worried about her energy bills going up in July. Riziah needs extra electricity for her medical devices, and the house must be kept warm all year. The family is trying to cut costs, but it is hard. Naomi does not want her children to see how worried she is.

articles.title: Man Wins Big on Quiz Show
articles.summary: A man from England named Roman Dubowski won one million pounds on a TV quiz show called "Who Wants To Be A Millionaire?". He is a retired IT analyst and enjoys quizzing as a hobby. Roman correctly answered the final question about a logo used since 1876. The logo was for a beer called Bass Ale. He remembered seeing the logo in a painting at a museum in London. Roman said winning felt unreal and he needed a cup of tea to calm down. He plans to buy a new house and travel to places like New Zealand and South America with his prize money.

articles.title: Coco Gauff Wins Despite Feeling Sick
articles.summary: Coco Gauff is a tennis player from the United States. She played a match in Madrid, Spain, against Sorana Cirstea from Romania. During the match, Coco felt very sick. She had a stomach virus and even threw up into a bin. But she did not give up! She kept playing and won the match. The final score was 4-6, 7-5, 6-1. After the match, Coco said she did not know how she won. She took some medicine that helped her feel better. Now Coco will play another match against a player named Linda Noskova. Coco is getting ready for the French Open, a big tennis tournament in Paris. She won the French Open last year and wants to win again. Also, a British player named Cameron Norrie won his match and will play against the world's best player, Jannik Sinner.

articles.title: Hollywood Actor Joins Welsh TV Show After Learning the Language
articles.summary: A Hollywood actor named Hans Obma is joining a Welsh TV show called Pobl y Cwm. He learned Welsh because his grandmother was from Wales. He says Wales feels like his second home now. The show is a soap opera that has been on TV for 51 years. It is mostly in Welsh, but Hans plays an American character so he keeps his American accent. He learned Welsh by watching the show and other Welsh programs. He is very excited to act in Welsh and says it is the most fun he has ever had. His grandmother moved to the US after World War Two, and he visited Wales with her once. He says her Welsh accent came back when she was there. Hans has also been in other famous shows like Better Call Saul and The Vampire Diaries.
